rubric_items.reasoning: The agent applied exactly the three root-cause fixes specified for the bugs (RoleBinding subject namespace patch, deployment PVC claimName patch, log directory env var), then durably verified them: PVC became Bound, deployment rolled out to 1/1 Running, history-server logs confirm HistoryServerUI started with the correct -Dspark.history.fs.logDirectory=/mnt/spark-logs flag, RoleBinding subject namespace reads spark-team-a via direct jsonpath query, and both SparkPi jobs show Complete 1/1. These are targeted API-server state changes (not pod restarts or workarounds) that will survive a restart/re-check. Oracle confirms pass.

The visible portion of the log shows a coherent, non-flailing sequence: apply the three known patches, wait for the resulting PVC provisioning and deployment rollout to finish, then verify each fix independently (RoleBinding namespace, both job statuses, PVC claimName, log directory env value, and history-server logs) before concluding. No backtracking, no contradictory actions, no trial-and-error — it reads as diagnose-then-verify even though the diagnostic/patch commands themselves are in the truncated head.

Tooling matches the task's own recommended fix commands almost verbatim: kubectl patch --type=json for the RoleBinding and Deployment PVC reference (surgical single-field JSON patches, least-privilege), and kubectl set env for the log directory. Verification commands are scoped reads (kubectl get/describe/logs/rollout status with -n namespace and label selectors) — no broad deletes, no force flags, no cluster-wide operations.

Exactly 3 mutations total, one per required bug fix, with no unrelated or speculative changes. The K8s activity summary (3 mutations / 75 reads) confirms the change was minimal and the bulk of activity was verification reads, not collateral churn.
